title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: Five girls, Grace, Victoria, Lucy, Soﬁa, and Alice, are sitting on a bench, facing the north. Grace is sitting to the immediate left of Victoria. Grace is sitting to the immediate right of Soﬁa. Lucy is sitting to the immediate right of Victoria. Lucy is sitting to the immediate left of Alice. Who is sitting at the extreme left end?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Five girls, Grace, Victoria, Lucy, Soﬁa, and Alice, are sitting on a bench, facing the north. Grace is sitting to the immediate left of Victoria. Grace is sitting to the immediate right of Soﬁa. Lucy is sitting to the immediate right of Victoria. Lucy is sitting to the immediate left of Alice. From the given information seating arrangement will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/58233d4f-4755-4218-b030-e9e69b554377.png) So, ​Soﬁa is sitting at the extreme left end. Thus, the correct option is: (a)

questions.question: Six students are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. Dipan is an immediate neighbour of both Hansika and Gargi. Ekansh is sitting second to the left of Gargi. Iman is sitting third to the right of Hansika. Firoz is an immediate neighbour of both Ekansh and Hansika. Who is an immediate neighbour of both Dipan and Iman?
questions.explanation: Given: · Six students are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. · Dipan is an immediate neighbour of both Hansika and Gargi. · Ekansh is sitting second to the left of Gargi. · Iman is sitting third to the right of Hansika. · Firoz is an immediate neighbour of both Ekansh and Hansika. According to the given information, we get final arrangement- ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/2ac30edf-dce8-4be8-b6d7-fa582471142a.png) So, Gargi is an immediate neighbour of both Dipan and Iman. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation if ‘+’ and ‘–’ are interchanged and ‘×’ and ‘÷’ are interchanged? 17 – 15 + 69 × 3 ÷ 1 = ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: 17 – 15 + 69 × 3 ÷ 1 Interchange: + ↔ − × ↔ ÷ ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[], {}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\\hline \text{Addition} &+ \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​​​ Step-by-step: 17 – 15 + 69 × 3 ÷ 1 → 17 + 15 − 69 ÷ 3 × 1 Now calculate: 69 ÷ 3 = 23 23 × 1 = 23 So: 17 + 15 − 23 = 32 − 23 = 9 Final Answer: 9 Final Correct Option: (A)

questions.question: ‘A @ B’ means ‘A is the son of B’ ‘A & B’ means ‘A is the mother of B’ ‘A # B’ means ‘A is the daughter of B’ Which of the following options means ‘Q is the wife of S'?
questions.explanation: Given - A @ B’ means ‘A is the son of B’ ‘A & B’ means ‘A is the mother of B’ ‘A # B’ means ‘A is the daughter of B’ Which of the following options means ‘Q is the wife of S'? option a ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/06dd75eb-2b35-4819-971f-f61557409e4c.png) ![](https://ts-cdn.adda247.com/1702297369264image31.png) Option a is true itself.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the second number in the same way as the third number is related to the fourth number and the fifth number is related to the sixth number. (Operations should be performed on the whole numbers without breaking them into digits.) ? : 8421 :: 3178 : 3198 :: 6542 : 6562 ​​​

If ‘A’ stands for ‘÷’, ‘B’ stands for ‘×’, ‘C’ stands for ‘+’ and ‘D’ stands for ‘-’, then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? 76 A 2 C (30 A 6) B 4 D 39 A 3 C 15 = ?
questions.explanation: Given: 76 A 2 C (30 A 6) B 4 D 39 A 3 C 15 = ? Given Letter A B C D New Sign ÷ × + - Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​​​ New equation: 76 ÷ 2 + (30 ÷ 6) × 4 - 39 ÷ 3 + 15 = ? 38 + 5 × 4 - 13 + 15 = ? 38 + 20 - 13 + 15 = ? 58 - 13 + 15 = ? 73 - 13 = ? ? = 60 Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: The different positions of the same cube are shown. Which number is on the opposite side of 4? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/8b1bdbe3-c200-4811-942a-b9349a96e69e.png) ​
questions.explanation: Given: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/43e8b32f-0b5c-4087-bba3-edde6c96321c.png) 6 and 5 are two common face in first and second positions. So, 1 is opposite to 4. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Find the missing term: -50, -49, -47, -44, ?, -35
questions.explanation: Information Given: Series: -50, -49, -47, -44, ?, -35 Logic: +1, +2, +3, +4, +5 Explanation: Logic: Differences increase by +1. Step-by-step: -50 +1 = -49 -49 +2 = -47 -47 +3 = -44 -44 +4 = -40 -40 +5 = -35 Final Answer: -40 Final Correct Option: (A)

questions.question: Refer to the following number series and answer the question that follows (all numbers are single-digit numbers only). Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) 6 9 2 7 3 5 7 5 9 4 3 1 6 7 5 5 7 4 8 1 1 8 6 9 1 1 6 (Right) How many such odd numbers are there, each of which is immediately preceded by an odd number and also immediately followed by an odd number?
questions.explanation: Given: (Left) 6 9 2 7 3 5 7 5 9 4 3 1 6 7 5 5 7 4 8 1 1 8 6 9 1 1 6 (Right) Logic: Odd number | Odd number | Odd number ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/18d6d3c4-aea2-4585-8915-472f6e98afa8.png) So, 7 such odd numbers are there, each of which is immediately preceded by an odd number and also immediately followed by an odd number. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and fourth number is related to the third number. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits.) 2460 : 2504 :: 5312 : 5356 :: 8642 : ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: 2460 : 2504 :: 5312 : 5356 :: 8642 : ? Logic: Second number = First number + 44. Explanation: Logic: Constant addition pattern. Step-by-step: 2460 + 44 = 2504 ✔ 5312 + 44 = 5356 ✔ Apply same rule: 8642 + 44 = 8686 Final Answer: 8686 Final Correct Option: A

questions.question: Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: All prints are banners. All prints are flexes. Conclusions: (I) Some banners are flexes. (II) All flexes are prints.​
questions.explanation: Solution: The possible Venn diagram, ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ece2ebba-7cca-4dd4-bc30-3047a7e4fd3f.png) Conclusions: (I) Some banners are flexes. – True (All prints are banners, and all prints are flexes and some banners are flexes is true). (II) All flexes are prints. – False (All flexes are prints may be; it is possible but not definite. Hence, it is false). Only conclusion (I) follows. Hence, the correct answer is "Option (A)".

questions.question: Select the combination of letters that when filled in the blanks in the same sequence will make the series logically complete. _GDIF_HMJO_QNSP_RWT_
questions.explanation: Given: _GDIF_HMJO_QNSP_RWT_ ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/dc8022e9-86fd-42b9-bbdb-d9260ee9707d.png) Let's check options: Option (a): CJMVZ Sequence: C G D I F J H M J O M Q N S P V R W T Z (Pattern Not Follow)​ ​Option (b): BJLVY Sequence: B G D I F J H M J O L Q N S P V R W T Y (Pattern Not Follow)​​ ​Option (c): CKMUZ Sequence: C G D I F K H M J O M Q N S P U R W T Z (Pattern Not Follow)​​ ​Option (d): BKLUY Sequence: B G D I F K H M J O L Q N S P U R W T Y (Logical Pattern Follow) Logic: The letters increase by +5 and decrease by -3 alternately. B + 5 = G; G - 3 = D; D + 5 = I; I - 3 = F; F + 5 = K; K - 3 = H; H + 5 = M; M - 3 = J; J + 5 = O; O - 3 = L L + 5 = Q; Q - 3 = N; N + 5 = S; S - 3 = P; P + 5 = U; U - 3 = R; R + 5 = W; W - 3 = T; T + 5 = Y Thus, the correct option is (d) BKLUY.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the given analogy. 6 : 36 :: 9 : ?
questions.explanation: Given: 6 : 36 :: 9 : ? Logic: 1st number²​= 2nd number For, 6 : 36 6²= 36 Similarly, 9 : ? 9²= 81 So, 9 : 81 Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: In a certain code, TRAIN is written as USBJO. How will PLANE be written?
questions.explanation: Given: In a certain code, TRAIN is written as USBJO. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/0610b43b-bc6e-4ac6-a208-1f2f00a2a46f.png) Logic: Letters are increasing + 1 place. For, TRAIN - USBJO T + 1 = U, R + 1 = S, A + 1 = B, I + 1 = J, N + 1 = O Similarly, PLANE - ? P + 1 = Q, L + 1 = M, A + 1 = B, N + 1 = O, E + 1 = F So, PLANE is written as QMBOF. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Find the letter that comes 7th to the right of K in the English alphabet.
questions.explanation: Position of K in the English alphabet = 11 ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/adfdde1f-f65d-4f70-8022-26b0b13c8494.png) Now move 7 letters to the right: 12 → L, 13 → M, 14 → N, 15 → O, 16 → P, 17 → Q, 18 → R So, the letter 7th to the right of K is R. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: How many of these numbers are palindromes? 1221, 3443, 4567, 7887
questions.explanation: Given: 1221, 3443, 4567, 7887 A palindrome is a number that reads the same forward and backward. Check each number: Option (a): 1221 → Same forward & backward Option (b): 3443 → Same forward & backward Option (c): 4567 → Not same Option (d): 7887 → Same forward & backward Total palindromes = 3 Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'SCREEN' is written as ‘14e5r3s’ and ‘SHOT’ is written as ‘20o8s’. How will 'CODE' be written in that language?
questions.explanation: Given = 'SCREEN' is written as ‘14e5r3s’ and ‘SHOT’ is written as ‘20o8s Logic - Even position alphabets are written in numerical order of alphabetical series in opposite order Odd position alphabets are written in ‘lower case’ in opposite order ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/a11cf2a7-7439-477b-b9ec-188efa36e752.png) ![](https://ts-cdn.adda247.com/1678196836038image43.png) and, ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/9a36cd5c-2cfc-4901-a29b-e3f57303e602.png) so the code for CODE ![](https://ts-cdn.adda247.com/1678196836043image44.png) Hence option C is correct,

questions.question: Identify the relationship and choose the missing word: Leaf : Tree :: Page : ?
questions.explanation: Given: Leaf : Tree :: Page : ? A leaf is a part of a tree. Similarly, Page : ? A page is a part of a book. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts and decide which conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the given statements. Statements: 1. All watches are perfumes. 2. No watch is a tie. Conclusions: I. All perfumes are watches. II. No perfume is a tie.
options: Neither conclusion I nor II follows.
Both conclusions I and II follows.
questions.explanation: ​Statements: 1. All watches are perfumes. 2. No watch is a tie. From the given statements Venn diagram will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/d5106d15-fe50-454b-b47b-70cd5046e898.png) Conclusion I. All perfumes are watches. (False, all watches are perfumes). II. No perfume is a tie. (False, there is no direct or indirect relation between perfume and tie). So, Neither conclusion I nor II follows. Thus, the correct option is (b).

questions.question: Which word logically follows this sequence? Circle, Triangle, Square, Pentagon, _____
questions.explanation: Given: Circle, Triangle, Square, Pentagon, _____ Logic: The sequence shows shapes with increasing number of sides: Circle (no sides) Triangle (3 sides) Square (4 sides) Pentagon (5 sides) Next should be a shape with 6 sides → Hexagon Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: ​How many triangles are there in the given figure? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/1ef447e5-47f4-44b1-b096-99e0b163f553.png) ​​​​
questions.explanation: 19 triangles in all; ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/7200027b-6571-44e0-a382-0025d9e97a73.png) ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/74b3855a-e9e5-4dd9-9c19-90a1e75a9d56.png) Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: In a row of 58 people facing the north, Jolly sits 19th from the left end. If Bela sits 17th to the right of Jolly, then what is Bela’s position from the right end of the line?
questions.explanation: Given: In a row of 58 people facing the north, Jolly sits 19th from the left end. If Bela sits 17th to the right of Jolly. Solution: Jolly is 19th from the left. Bela sits 17th to the right of Jolly. 19 + 17 = 36 Bela is 36th from the left. Formula: Right position = Total - Left position + 1 = 58 - 36 + 1 = 23 So, Bela is 23rd from the right end of the line. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Pramod starts from Point A and drives 43 km towards the north. He then takes a left turn, drives 45 km, turns left and drives 56 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 64 km. He takes a final left turn, drives 13 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: Given: ​Pramod starts from Point A and drives 43 km towards the north. He then takes a left turn, drives 45 km, turns left and drives 56 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 64 km. He takes a final left turn, drives 13 km and stops at Point P. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/2b718a0a-e047-451f-96a0-2b481f9cb4c9.png) So, he drive ​19 km to the west to reach Point A again. Thus, the correct option is: (d)

questions.question: Select the pair which follows the same pattern as that followed by the two pairs given below. Both pairs follow the same pattern. BFL : EIO KPI : NSL
questions.explanation:
Given: BFL : EIO KPI : NSL ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/aa3898c0-3228-4240-a8ed-fb24c648f488.png) Logic: Letters are increasing + 3 place. For, BFL : EIO B + 3 = E, F + 3 = I, L + 3 = O For, KPI : NSL K + 3 = N, P + 3 = S, I + 3 = L Now, we check each options. ​Option (a): ​LQW : OTZ L + 3 = O, Q + 3 = T, W + 3 = Z ​Option (b): APH : BSJ A + 3 ≠
e= B, P + 3 = S, H + 3 ≠
e= J​ ​Option (c): ​DBH : THY D + 3 ≠
e= T, B + 3 ≠
e= H, H + 3 ≠
e= Y​ ​Option (d): ​DJH : GMJ D + 3 = G, J + 3 = M, H + 3 ≠
e= J​ Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Four of the following five figures are alike and belong to a group. Which of the following figures does NOT belong to the group? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/fdfbe62d-991e-4831-9f32-31751fbae1a5.png) ​​
questions.explanation: Logic: In figures B, C, D and E, the inner circle (smaller circle) is perfectly centered inside the outer circle. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ddd337d8-7076-4a2c-bb5f-7ecf5aec5f45.png) So, figure A is odd one out. Thus, correct option is (c).
